2604.12986.title: Parallax: Why AI Agents That Think Must Never Act
2604.12986.paper_summary: Autonomous AI agents are rapidly transitioning from experimental tools to operational infrastructure, with projections that 80% of enterprise applications will embed AI copilots by the end of 2026. As agents gain the ability to execute real-world actions (reading files, running commands, making network requests, modifying databases), a fundamental security gap has emerged. The dominant approach to agent safety relies on prompt-level guardrails: natural language instructions that operate at the same abstraction level as the threats they attempt to mitigate. This paper argues that prompt-based safety is architecturally insufficient for agents with execution capability and introduces Parallax, a paradigm for safe autonomous AI execution grounded in four principles: Cognitive-Executive Separation, which structurally prevents the reasoning system from executing actions; Adversarial Validation with Graduated Determinism, which interposes an independent, multi-tiered validator between reasoning and execution; Information Flow Control, which propagates data sensitivity labels through agent workflows to detect context-dependent threats; and Reversible Execution, which captures pre-destructive state to enable rollback when validation fails. We present OpenParallax, an open-source reference implementation in Go, and evaluate it using Assume-Compromise Evaluation, a methodology that bypasses the reasoning system entirely to test the architectural boundary under full agent compromise. Across 280 adversarial test cases in nine attack categories, Parallax blocks 98.9% of attacks with zero false positives under its default configuration, and 100% of attacks under its maximum-security configuration. When the reasoning system is compromised, prompt-level guardrails provide zero protection because they exist only within the compromised system; Parallax's architectural boundary holds regardless.
2604.12986.paper_summary_zh: 自主AI代理正在迅速從實驗工具轉變為操作基礎設施，預計到2026年底，80%的企業應用將嵌入AI副駕駛。隨著代理獲得執行現實世界行動的能力（讀取文件、執行命令、發送網絡請求、修改數據庫），一個根本的安全漏洞出現了。當前對代理安全的主導方法依賴於提示級別的防護措施：自然語言指令在與它們試圖緩解的威脅相同的抽象層次上運作。本文主張，基於提示的安全對於具有執行能力的代理在架構上是不足的，並引入Parallax，一種基於四個原則的安全自主AI執行範式：認知-執行分離，結構性地防止推理系統執行行動；漸進決定論的對抗驗證，在推理和執行之間插入一個獨立的多層驗證器；信息流控制，通過代理工作流程傳播數據敏感性標籤以檢測上下文依賴的威脅；以及可逆執行，捕捉破壞前狀態以便在驗證失敗時啟用回滾。我們展示了OpenParallax，一個用Go實現的開源參考實現，並使用假設妥協評估進行評估，這是一種完全繞過推理系統以測試在完全代理妥協下的架構邊界的方法。在九個攻擊類別中的280個對抗性測試案例中，Parallax在其默認配置下阻止了98.9%的攻擊，且沒有假陽性，在其最大安全配置下則阻止了100%的攻擊。當推理系統被妥協時，提示級別的防護措施提供零保護，因為它們僅存在於被妥協的系統內；Parallax的架構邊界則不受影響。
2604.12986.author: Joel Fokou et.al.
